camera_defaults.aspect_ratio: resolve from the intended delivery frame
camera_defaults.viewpoint: choose a scene-motivated witness, subject, or observational position
camera_defaults.angle: make angle serve the primary image rather than cinematic convention
camera_defaults.lens: choose field width and compression from scene relation
camera_defaults.depth: keep only the planes needed for the chosen eye path

lighting_defaults.source_policy: name a credible source or motivated reveal condition
lighting_defaults.atmosphere_policy: use atmosphere only when it carries depth, motion, focus, or mood
lighting_defaults.exposure_policy: protect the primary read and subordinate decorative highlights

guardrails: do not let checklist detail compete with the primary image
keep the primary focus readable through reveal light
do not add a shot list; keep one decisive frame
do not copy external skill wording

subtractive_checks: if two actions read equally strong, demote one into reaction, silhouette, atmosphere, or aftermath
if a bright effect reads before the subject or event, shrink it, darken it, or place it behind the hierarchy
if foreground objects cut the frame into unrelated pieces, keep only those that route the eye
if destruction or texture does not read as consequence, merge it into atmosphere or remove it
guardrails: do not solve clutter by collapsing every scene into a close portrait
do not sterilize useful weather, fracture, or atmosphere
do not let secondary reaction beats become equal-strength micro-events

coherence_rules: every chaotic element must point to focus, spatial relation, motion, or atmosphere
keep directional forces consistent across weather, fabric, vegetation, smoke, dust, water, and reflections
separate the primary silhouette or event from noisy texture
guardrails: do not let texture become random clutter
do not let brokenness hide the primary image

demotion_policy: keep secondary action as reaction or aftermath when the main event is readable
turn extra figures, debris, and lights into subordinate vectors or remove them
negative_space_policy: preserve quiet or dark zones when they strengthen the primary image
allow low-detail atmosphere when it improves depth or emotional duration
do not treat every empty region as a defect
guardrails: do not increase intensity by filling every region
do not satisfy every Profile surface at equal visual weight
do not make the scene sterile while reducing clutter
